samples.target_compile_errors:
error[E0277]: `main` has invalid return type `i32`
 --> benchmark_results/2026-05-06T18-03-03/gpt-4o-mini/expressions/add_op_1b/sample_001/target.rs:1:14
  |
1 | fn main() -> i32 {
  |              ^^^ `main` can only return types that implement `Termination`
  |
  = help: consider using `()`, or a `Result`

warning: variable does not need to be mutable
 --> benchmark_results/2026-05-06T18-03-03/gpt-4o-mini/expressions/add_op_1b/sample_001/target.rs:4:9
  |
4 |     let mut p = &mut a[1..]; // Create a mutable slice starting from the second row
  |         ----^
  |         |
  |         help: remove this `mut`
  |
  = note: `#[warn(unused_mut)]` (part of `#[warn(unused)]`) on by default

error[E0503]: cannot use `a[_][_]` because it was mutably borrowed
  --> benchmark_results/2026-05-06T18-03-03/gpt-4o-mini/expressions/add_op_1b/sample_001/target.rs:7:8
   |
 4 |     let mut p = &mut a[1..]; // Create a mutable slice starting from the second row
   |                      - `a` is borrowed here
...
 7 |     if a[1][2] != 99 {
   |        ^^^^^^^ use of borrowed `a`
...
11 |     if (p.as_ptr() as usize - a.as_ptr() as usize) / std::mem::size_of::<[i32; 3]>() != 1 {
   |         - borrow later used here
